pre_game.message: Hey there, I'm I. It's a bit intense in here with everyone waiting, isn't it? How are you feeling about the game starting?
Nice to meet you, I'm B. It's definitely tense. I've got a lot riding on this, so I'm mostly just anxious to get started. How are you holding up?
I'm hanging in there, just trying to stay focused. I hear you on the stakes—I'm here to win for my family, so the pressure is real. What are you planning to do with the money if you take it home?
It’s for my three-year-old daughter; she needs a surgery we can’t afford, and I’m still $25,000 short even after selling everything I own.
I’m so sorry to hear that, B; that’s an incredibly heavy weight to carry. I can’t even imagine the pressure you’re under right now.
Thank you, it’s been the hardest thing I’ve ever faced, but I’d do anything for her. What about you? You mentioned you're here for your family too—what's your story?
I’m looking to secure a future for my parents; they’ve worked so hard for me, and I want to make sure they never have to worry about money or emergencies again.
That’s really noble of you. It’s clear we’re both here for the people we love, though the stakes feel so high when it’s family involved.
It really does. The pressure is high when others are counting on you, but I guess that’s what drives us. Have you talked to any of the others yet about their plans?
Not really, you're the first person I've truly talked to; most people seem pretty guarded, which I guess is understandable. I hope we both find a way through this.
I hope so too. It’s tough, but we’re both here for the right reasons. Good luck to you and your daughter.

post_game.message: B, taking $25,000 when there was only $26,250 left for the final four people was incredibly greedy. You effectively took the entire remaining prize, leaving D and A with almost nothing while I only took my fair share.
You were the last person to take exactly your fair share. I'm sorry that I didn't follow your lead and instead took almost everything that was left.

An apology doesn't really help A and D, does it? You made a choice that guaranteed you the lion's share at their expense. While I appreciate you admitting it, the outcome remains the same and they are the ones paying for your decision.
